cover.title: THE OCEAN
cover.subtitle: The one who opens the surface easily, while keeping the deepest room locked.
cover.tagline: You can get close to anyone, but depth is the door you rarely open.

profile_scores.caption: High drive and stability, low competitiveness and pride. You get close to anyone easily, but the deepest part stays locked separately.

short_summary: You can get close quickly, warmly, and naturally, but that doesn't mean you've let someone all the way in.
Your loneliness doesn't come from having too few people. It comes from keeping the deepest room untouched.
What changes love for you is not opening wider, but opening one place deeply.

full_report.headline: THE OCEAN
full_report.intro: You're someone who makes friends easily. No matter who you meet, conversation flows and people get comfortable fast. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why surface closeness happens quickly for you, why the deepest room stays locked, and how one relationship can finally move beyond the surface.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're someone who makes friends easily.
No matter who you meet, conversation flows, and you get comfortable fast.
People look at you and think, "this person really gets along with everyone."
But here's the thing. Getting along with people and actually letting someone in are completely different things to you.
To you, this isn't hiding. You just feel like they're naturally different territories.
You stay close with a lot of people, but almost no one has ever actually made it all the way in.
It's the same when you love.
At first, you click, you're comfortable, and you get close fast.
But the moment a relationship is about to go deeper, you still look like you're getting along fine. You just never open the innermost room.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Stability, Low Competitiveness · Low Pride. You get close to anyone easily, but the deepest part stays locked separately.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat: THE SURFACE OCEAN CURVE
body: You're someone who gets close faster than almost anyone, early in a relationship.
Conversation flows, the vibe feels natural, the other person gets comfortable fast.
The problem starts after that.
The deeper the relationship gets, the more it feels like you're showing more, but in fact there's one core part you never touch.
The other person expects, "we've gotten this close, so surely they'll show me the real inside now."
But you always stop right at that line.
On the surface it keeps looking more and more intimate, but the distance to the core stays exactly the same as it was at the start.
chapters.highlight: The reason you feel lonely isn't that you lack people. It's that you've never shown anyone your deepest room.

chart.title: THE SURFACE OCEAN CURVE
chart.description: The pattern where the surface gets close fast early on, but even as the relationship goes on, the distance to the core never shrinks, and only the surface intimacy keeps widening.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: True. But getting along well and being close are different things. You're great at getting along, but that's not proof of depth.
items.truth_short: Getting along well is not the same thing as real depth.

items.truth: Hmm. You don't think you're hiding. You just think of it as a separate area. But the other person senses that door has never once been opened.
items.truth_short: You may not feel hidden, but the deepest door still hasn't opened.

items.belief: I meet so many people, so I'm not lonely.
items.truth: No. Meeting a lot of people and not being lonely are different things. Your loneliness doesn't come from a lack of people. It comes from a lack of depth.
items.truth_short: People can be everywhere, and depth can still be missing.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: They're all different people.
But strangely, people with a similar texture keep coming into your life.
Someone who doesn't ask about depth. Someone satisfied with the current level of closeness. Someone who doesn't try to get any closer.
It's comfortable for you, so you end up maintaining that exact distance.
chapters.highlight: Someone who feels comfortable with you sees your breadth. Someone who loves you notices the locked room inside you.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to make even more friends.
You're already too good at that.
What you actually need is to open the locked room just a little, for one single person.
You don't need to show everything.
Just one thing you've never said before.
Sounds like nothing, right? But for you, that's the deepest step you can take.
chapters.action_sentence: For you, depth doesn't begin by opening to everyone. It begins by opening one locked place to one person.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You already know how to be comfortable with anyone.
You know it very well.
But what you'll learn next is how to go past the surface for one person.
Not opening wide. Opening one place deeply.
That might actually feel scarier than you'd think.
But considering all the wide relationships you've already built, you can absolutely learn this.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time learning how to be comfortable with anyone. Now you're learning how to go deeper with one person.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone who doesn't ask about depth
items.description: They enjoy your company exactly as it is. No difficult questions. No curiosity about what's underneath. Months pass comfortably, and neither of you notices how little of yourself you've actually shared.

items.type_title: Someone who likes conversation more than questions
items.description: You stay up talking for hours. Stories, jokes, ideas. But whenever the conversation gets personal, they smoothly move on. The connection feels wide, yet never becomes deep.

items.type_title: Someone with no secrets of their own
items.description: They tell you everything on the first few dates. Family stories, old heartbreaks, future dreams. Because they're open, they assume you've been equally open too. But your deepest room remains untouched.

items.type_title: Someone who mistakes frequency for intimacy
items.description: You meet three times a week. You text every day. They proudly say, "Nobody knows me like you do." Yet inside, you know they've never seen the part of you that matters most.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs actually showed up pretty early. It's just that every time, you told yourself "this is enough" and moved on.

signal_strength.label: You get close fast, but barely have any deep conversations
The relationship has lasted a while, but doesn't feel much deeper
The other person doesn't really notice either, because the surface is already warm enough

chapters.core_signal: The other person believes they know you well, but the truth is, you've never once shown them your core.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They wait without demanding depth.
checklist.description: They don't rush. They wait until you open up.

checklist.title: They tell the difference between surface and real intimacy.
checklist.description: They see getting along with everyone the same way as something shown only to you, differently.

checklist.title: They don't find it heavy when you show your core.
checklist.description: When you open something up, they don't take it too heavily.

checklist.title: Being together makes things deeper.
checklist.description: It doesn't stop at the surface. You go in further together.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You almost never fail at getting close on the surface. The problem is you never go further than that. So there's someone who doesn't fit you well: someone satisfied with surface-level intimacy who doesn't want any more depth. They think the current level is fine. If so, the relationship stays on the surface forever.
sections.who_steadies_you: On the other hand, there's someone who unexpectedly fits you well. Someone who notices what's beyond the surface, and waits without pushing. Someone who says "I want to know more" without forcing you. In front of someone like that, you can open the locked room more safely.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You might be drawn again to someone who doesn't ask about depth. You might fall for someone who only likes your popularity. That can't really be stopped. That's just who you are. The problem isn't being drawn to them. It's how long you stay only on the surface, after that.
sections.next_signs: Remember the signs from chapter 08? They usually didn't show up until months in. This time, they'll show up faster, and smaller. The moment a deep question comes up. Normally you'd subtly change the subject. Right before that, there's a brief pause. That's where it starts.
sections.try_this: Normally, you would've changed the subject. This time, don't. Just once. Bring up one thing you've never said before, just as it is. Then watch their reaction. Someone who can take it listens quietly, without brushing it off. Someone who can't treats it like it's nothing, or changes the subject again.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to create wide connection. What's left is learning how to open one place deeply.
action_items: This week, tell one person something you've never said before.
Notice when you change the subject right before a deeper question lands.
Choose one relationship where depth matters more than frequency.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to be comfortable with anyone. This report covered why you've always stopped at the surface, and how you can learn to go deeper with one person.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
